title: Molten Russian Beef Dumplings
description: A comforting Russian dish featuring garlicky beef dumplings cooked to perfection on a robata grill, infused with the rich flavor of Shaoxing wine. This low-calorie-friendly recipe is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, Shaoxing wine, garlic, onion, salt, and black pepper, and mix well with your hands until just combined.
Gradually add the flour to the beef mixture, and mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape, and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Brush the dumplings with the beaten egg, and place them on the preheated robata grill.
Cook the dumplings for 5 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
